key_insight: A single crowder density can drive a polymer through a coilâ†’globuleâ†’coil reentrant transition because attractive crowders first bridge monomers and then saturate binding sites, breaking the bridges at high density.
engineering_relevance: This identifies saturable bridging as a minimal design rule for tuning polymer compaction and re-expansion in crowded soft-matter, biomaterial, and charged-polymer systems.
method: Molecular dynamics simulations of coarse-grained polymers with one species of attractive crowder
Sweep crowder volume fraction Ď†c to track conformational changes
Compare against purely repulsive crowders and charged polymers with explicit counterions
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that size distributions and conformational statistics show a full reentrant trajectory with attractive crowders, while repulsive crowders give only monotonic compaction and SAW-like behavior.
watch_item: The mechanism depends on model assumptions about coarse-graining and binding saturation, so real systems may shift quantitatively if crowder chemistry, stiffness, or solvent effects differ.
paper_title: Density-driven reentrant polymer transitions via saturable bridging crowders
